definition: A named unit of recurring background work carrying a cron expression, created by an operator and executed as runs.
usage_context: Requirements FR-001–FR-007, Acceptance Scenarios, queue records, generated entity and endpoint names
source_locations: job-scheduler-spec.md — FR-001–FR-007; AS2; AS3 (vs. "task" in AS1, Edge Cases)

definition: The queue that holds jobs awaiting execution.
usage_context: AS1 queue placement; Edge Cases outage behavior

definition: A single execution of a job.
usage_context: AS2, AS3 execution behavior; FR-004 results; FR-005 leases; Success Criteria
source_locations: job-scheduler-spec.md — AS2; AS3; FR-004; FR-005; Success Criteria

definition: The recurrence rule attached to a job at creation; validated before the job is accepted.
usage_context: FR-001 job creation; FR-002 validation ("cron" acceptable shorthand after the qualified first use)

definition: Operator-initiated stop of a job: the current run finishes its current step, then the job moves to status "cancelled". Explicitly does not kill an in-flight process.
usage_context: AS2 operator action; FR-007 boundary with kill

definition: Runner-initiated termination of an in-flight process
forbidden_variants: AS3 lease expiry; FR-007 boundary with cancel

definition: The configurable duration for which a completed run's results stay cached.
usage_context: FR-004 caching; Success Criteria serving rule
forbidden_variants: TTL (bare, when the referent is not already established)

definition: The duration of the lease each run holds; extendable at most twice, and expiry triggers kill.
forbidden_variants: TTL (bare, when the referent is not already established)
